title: Claude Fable 5
summary:
Anthropic has launched Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5, "Mythos-class" models representing a significant leap in AI capabilities. Fable 5 is available for general use, showcasing state-of-the-art performance across software engineering, knowledge work, vision, scientific research, and long-context understanding. It can perform multi-month codebase migrations in a day, ace senior-level financial analysis, rebuild web apps from screenshots, and autonomously play complex games like Pokémon and Factorio.

To manage the inherent risks of such powerful AI, Fable 5 is equipped with robust, albeit conservatively tuned, safeguards. Queries related to cybersecurity, biology/chemistry, or "distillation" are automatically redirected to Claude Opus 4.8, affecting less than 5% of sessions.

Mythos 5 is the same underlying model as Fable 5, but with lifted safeguards in specific areas. Initially deployed through Project Glasswing with the US government for cyberdefenders, Mythos 5 possesses the strongest cybersecurity capabilities globally. It has also accelerated drug design tenfold, generated novel molecular biology hypotheses, and conducted autonomous genomics research outperforming published models.

Both models are priced at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens, significantly lower than previous versions, aiming for broader access. Early feedback highlights Fable 5's autonomy, senior-level reasoning, and ability to tackle complex, long-horizon problems, marking it as a substantial advance over prior models.

title: xAI is looking more like a datacentre REIT than a frontier lab
summary:
xAI, now part of SpaceX, is increasingly resembling a datacentre REIT due to lucrative partnerships leasing its compute capacity to Anthropic and Google. This unexpected move follows Anthropic's severe compute shortages, which had forced them to implement usage restrictions on their Claude products.

xAI's Colossus 1 datacentre capacity has provided a lifeline, with Anthropic reportedly paying $1.25 billion/month for 300MW (220k GPUs) and Google $920 million/month for 110k GPUs. These deals are deemed "ludicrously profitable," potentially recouping xAI's capital expenditure within 18 months, with power costs being a minimal expense.

While motives like financial engineering for SpaceX's IPO or Elon Musk's rivalry with OpenAI are considered, the article emphasizes the acute, global shortage of GPUs as the primary driver. A significant competitive advantage for xAI/SpaceX is their unparalleled ability to rapidly build large-scale infrastructure, contrasting with the often-delayed projects of hyperscalers.

This pivot raises questions for Grok, xAI's own AI model, as capacity intended for its training and inference is now leased to competitors, suggesting a potential retreat from its ambition as a frontier lab. Ultimately, the article concludes that a mix of financial engineering, the severe compute shortage, and xAI's construction expertise are all shaping its evolving identity.

title: Siri AI
summary:
Apple introduces Siri AI and Apple Intelligence, a next-generation, truly helpful AI experience centered around the user's needs, integrated across apps, and deeply private. Coming later this year, with Siri AI in English initially.

The reimagined Siri AI is a more capable, conversational assistant that understands personal context, allowing users to ask open-ended questions, brainstorm, and take action across apps like Messages and Reminders, leveraging broad world knowledge. A new Siri app consolidates conversations, and Siri gains enhanced presence in CarPlay and Camera (Siri mode with Visual Intelligence).

Visual Intelligence expands to iPad, Mac, and Apple Vision Pro, enabling visual search, actions like splitting bills, identifying objects, and extracting information from screenshots. Photo editing receives a boost with Spatial Reframing, Extend, and Clean Up tools. Generative AI tools like Image Playground and Image Wand allow for creating unique images and transforming sketches.

Writing tools empower Siri to generate drafts, provide feedback, proofread, and match writing style across apps. Other new features include Live Translation, enhanced Safari functionalities, smarter Home app capabilities, Genmoji creation, and accessibility improvements.

Crucially, Apple Intelligence prioritizes privacy through on-device processing and Private Cloud Compute, ensuring personal data remains protected and never stored, even for complex server-based requests. The new capabilities are compatible with a wide range of recent iPhone, iPad, and Mac models, and Apple Vision Pro. Developers can also integrate these AI features into their apps.

title: MiMo-v2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed: 1T model with 1000 tokens per second
summary:
Xiaomi, in collaboration with TileRT, has unveiled MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter (1T) AI model capable of achieving an unprecedented 1000 tokens per second (TPS) generation speed on commodity GPUs. Announced on June 8, 2026, this breakthrough significantly elevates AI reasoning capabilities, enabling real-time, instantaneous responses for complex tasks.

Access to the MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed API is application-based and limited to a two-week promotional trial from June 9 to June 23, 2026. Priced at three times the cost of MiMo-V2.5-Pro, it promises approximately ten times the generation speed, with approved users also receiving free chat access during the trial.

This speed is touted as a paradigm shift, transforming AI applications. It facilitates advanced reasoning paths like Best-of-N and Tree Search, dramatically boosts coding agent productivity by eliminating latency bottlenecks, and enables 1T models to participate in time-critical real-time decision loops, such as high-frequency trading, anti-fraud, and life-saving surgical assistance.

The remarkable performance is a result of "extreme model-system codesign." On the model side, MiMo employs FP4 quantization for MoE Experts to reduce memory footprint and bandwidth, coupled with DFlash, an innovative block-level masked parallel prediction for speculative decoding that maximizes accepted tokens per verification round. TileRT's contribution includes an ultra-low-latency inference system with a revolutionary execution model, featuring Persistent Engine Kernels and Warp Specialization, meticulously optimized to eliminate execution gaps and perfectly align with MiMo's algorithms. This synergy allows the 1T model to achieve 1000+ TPS on a single standard 8-GPU commodity node.

For community engagement, Xiaomi has open-sourced the MiMo-V2.5-Pro-FP4-DFlash checkpoint on HuggingFace.
